description: A sharp drop exposes the steep or near-vertical face called 'pareshi', made of rock, earth, or ice. Cliffs can form beside water, across a valley, on a mountain, or at a glacier. 'shuna' (edge) names the boundary line; the cliff is the face and change of height.
articulatory_notes: Close the lips for unvoiced 'p' and release into central 'a'. Tap or briefly trill the tongue at the alveolar ridge before stressed middle-height front 'e'. Raise the tongue blade towards the postalveolar region for unvoiced 'sh' and finish with high-front unrounded 'i'.

semantic_domains.nature: an exposed steep face of rock, earth, or ice
semantic_domains.spatial: a sharp vertical change between higher and lower terrain
